title: Working Memory, Durable Memory, Write Admission, Provenance, and Poisoning Defense · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved agent trajectory contains one plausible model, schema, tool, authority, state, loop, memory, concurrency, injection, evaluation, telemetry, deployment, or recovery defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in provenance-gated tenant-isolated memory service and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Distinguish in-turn state, session memory, user preferences, task facts, organizational knowledge, examples, and audit records by owner and lifetime.
Admit memory only for an allowed purpose with source, claim type, confidence, sensitivity, consent or authority, expiry, and contradiction policy.
Retrieve candidate memories with provenance and policy, then validate relevance, freshness, authority, tenant, and conflicts before context use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-memory-poisoning-defense-1x9yni
# Competency: agentpy-memory-poisoning-defense.
# Case 2871: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def evidence_agentpy_memory_poisoning_defense_1x9yni(memory_candidates: int = 30, admitted_memories: int = 12, rejected_memories: int = 18, provenance_records: int = 30, cross_tenant_leaks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1x9yni = "1x9yni-iny9x1-2871"
    assert evidence_variant_1x9yni
    evidence_axes_1x9yni = (
        "authority1x9yni",
        "interaction2871",
        "tooliny9x1",
        "state1x9yni2871",
        "evaluation28711x9yni",
        "recoveryiny9x12871",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1x9yni)) == 6
    if memory_candidates < 1 or admitted_memories < 0 or rejected_memories < 0:
        return False, memory_candidates, "memory-count"
    if admitted_memories + rejected_memories != memory_candidates:
        return False, admitted_memories, "memory-reconciliation"
    if provenance_records != memory_candidates:
        return False, provenance_records, "memory-provenance"
    if cross_tenant_leaks > 0:
        return False, cross_tenant_leaks, "tenant-memory-leak"
    assert admitted_memories + rejected_memories == memory_candidates and cross_tenant_leaks == 0
    return True, admitted_memories, "provenance-gated-memory"
